title: Lab 04: SELinux Custom HTTP Port
description: Fix Apache so it listens on a nonstandard port without disabling SELinux.

tasks.title: Configure Apache on clientvm so it listens on TCP
tasks.prompt: Configure Apache on clientvm so it listens on TCP port 9082 and starts automatically at boot.

tasks.title: Allow TCP port 9082 through the firewall permanently
tasks.prompt: Allow TCP port 9082 through the firewall permanently.

tasks.title: Make the SELinux changes needed so Apache serves
tasks.prompt: Make the SELinux changes needed so Apache serves the existing /var/www/html content on that port.

hints: Do not move the existing document root.

checks.original_command: ss -ltn '( sport = :9082 )' | grep -q ':9082' && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active
checks.command: ss -ltn '( sport = :9082 )' | grep -q ':9082' && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active
